SEATTLE, Oct. l5.-Northern Pacific
officials have been notified that the
Burlington will immediately begin turn
ing over cars at Billings to be used
in handling shipments of lumber and
shingles. This action of the Burlington
will have perceptible effect upon the
shortage in the northwest, for while
it will not entirely relieve the situa
ton it vii go long way toward at
fording the facilities lumber and shin
gle. manufacturers require.


The car shortage has been felt most
acutely along the Burlington and Oh
other lines tapping Colorado. Nebraska
and Kansas. While the northern roads
have been short of ears, they have been
Athletic keep the mill men fairly well
supplied through the favorable arrange
ment made with connecting lines. The
Burlington has heretofore refused to
furnish any ears, and the Northern Pa
cfic has been unable to let its equip
ment go.


The eastern lines connecting witt
the Northern Pacific and Great North
ern havc been either providing then
own cars for hauling lumber and shin
